# @zakkster/lite-leakforge

> Leak specimens, CI harness, and diagnostic toolkit for @zakkster/lite-leak.

## What this package does

lite-leakforge is the product layer above lite-leak's leak tracker.
It provides three capabilities:

1. SPECIMENS: Deterministic leak scenarios that trigger each of
   lite-leak's six kernels. Each specimen declares expected events
   across three detection channels (FR leak reports, warnings,
   findings). The verify() contract diffs expected vs actual.

2. CI HARNESS: assertNoLeaks(fn) and leakSuite() for node:test.
   Exit codes: 0=clean, 1=confirmed leak, 3=inconclusive.
   Precedence: leaks/findings -> 1 even if FR did not settle;
   3 only for evidence-free unsettled runs. Requires --expose-gc.
   settleFinalizers() handles FinalizationRegistry non-determinism
   with bounded retry. Kernel teardown is exception-safe (try/finally
   around user code).

3. FORMATTERS + DASHBOARD: ASCII formatters for leak reports,
   findings, warnings, and owner paths. Dashboard data model with
   ghost-safe signal design (exactly 2 signals, zero per-event churn)
   and LAZY entry formatting (text/ownerPath/label are memoized
   getters -- zero formatting cost per event). createDashboard()
   renders the newest maxLogRows as a sliding window behind a
   dirty flag + power-of-2 frame mask.

## Key exports

```
import { assertNoLeaks, leakSuite, createLeakGate } from '@zakkster/lite-leakforge/harness';
import { verify, composeScenario, createTimerOrphanSpecimen } from '@zakkster/lite-leakforge/scenarios';
import { formatReport, formatOwnerPath, summarize } from '@zakkster/lite-leakforge/formatters';
import { createDashboardModel } from '@zakkster/lite-leakforge/panels';
```

## Specimen names

raw-fr, timer-orphan, listener-orphan, observer-orphan,
detached-dom, async-retention.

## Dependencies

- @zakkster/lite-leak ^1.0.0 (runtime)
- @zakkster/lite-signal >=1.5.0-beta.3 <2.0.0 (peer)

## Conventions

- Single-file ESM modules, MIT license, sideEffects: false
- node:test only (never vitest)
- ASCII-only source (no box-drawing chars in output)
- Verify contract runs: inject -> audit -> release -> settle
- verify() THROWS for needsSettle specimens without --expose-gc
  (env error, not a detection FAIL); pre-FR specimens run anywhere
- Subpath exports ship their own .d.ts files
